description: A plain candid documentary photograph, not a designed graphic. It is a vertical (portrait) snapshot of a dark-colored pickup truck lying completely upside-down (overturned, roof on the ground) in a grassy field/roadside, with its undercarriage, wheels and tires facing up toward the sky. The grass is tall and green, the lighting is flat daylight. The image is somewhat low-resolution and clearly a quick on-scene phone photo. There is NO on-image text, no agency seal/logo, no headline, slogan, or graphic-design typography overlaid. The OCR text is spurious garbage/coordinate noise, confirming there is no real designed text. The photo accompanies a news-style tweet describing Dallas ICE officers rescuing a driver from a pickup that lost a tire, rolled over seven times, and landed upside down on April 26. Intent is to portray ICE officers as heroic rescuers, but the image itself is undesigned.
